title: 11 Herbal Creams For Feverish Feeling
intro_desc: Herbal creams and teas are natural remedies that can help relieve feverish feelings. These herbs work by stimulating the body's immune system, reducing inflammation, and promoting sweating, which helps to cool down the body. For example, Echinacea purpurea is a popular herb used to boost the immune system, while Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to ease aches and pains. Eucalyptus globulus is another herb that can help to break down mucus and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe when you're feeling feverish.  Herbal teas are a great way to get these herbs into your system, as they can be easily absorbed by the body. Drinking a warm tea can also help to relax the body and mind, which can be especially helpful when you're feeling unwell. When you're feverish, your body temperature is higher than normal, and herbal teas can help to bring it back down to a healthy level. By using herbal creams and teas, you can avoid taking medication and instead choose a natural approach to feeling better.  Using herbal creams and teas can also bring many benefits to your life. For one, they're often free from harsh chemicals and artificial ingredients found in over-the-counter medications. This makes them a great option for people who are sensitive to certain ingredients or prefer a more natural approach to health. Additionally, herbal creams and teas can be used in combination with other natural remedies, such as rest, hydration, and a healthy diet, to help your body recover from illness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains a rich mixture of active constituents, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polyphenols. These compounds work together to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, which helps to alleviate feverish feelings. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to lower body temperature and reduce discomfort. Caffeic acid derivatives, on the other hand, have antioxidant properties that can help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, further contributing to the reduction of fever. The polyphenols in Echinacea purpurea creams also have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote overall health.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for feverish feeling
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Dry the Echinacea flowers in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours or air-dry them for 24 hours.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl in a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Echinacea flowers, shea butter, and vitamin E oil to the melted coconut oil mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature. Store the cream in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects, helping to reduce fever and alleviate discomfort. Gingerol and shogaol work by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and substances that contribute to fever. The warming effect of Zingiber officinale creams also helps to increase blood circulation, which can aid in the relief of feverish feelings. By targeting the underlying causes of fever, Zingiber officinale creams can help to provide relief and comfort.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for feverish feeling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of ginger essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove from heat and stir in the ginger essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof container and let it cool until solidified.
Apply a small amount of the Zingiber officinale cream to the affected area to help soothe a feverish feeling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains compounds like eucalyptol and cineole, which are known for their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. Eucalyptol helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, while cineole reduces swelling and relieves congestion in the airways. The cooling sensation of eucalyptus globulus creams can help to bring down a fever by causing the body to sweat, which cools the body and helps to eliminate toxins. The anti-inflammatory properties of eucalyptus globulus creams can also help to reduce body temperature by reducing inflammation in the body. Regular use of eucalyptus globulus creams may help to ease feverish feelings by providing relief from congestion and inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for feverish feeling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second intervals, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add 10 drops of eucalyptus essential oil to the melted mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a heat-resistant container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.
Once solid, store the eucalyptus cream in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks and use as needed to relieve feverish feelings.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains compounds like quinine, quinidine, and cinchonine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Quinine is a natural antipyretic that helps reduce fever by inhibiting the release of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause fever. Cinchonine, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate the discomfort associated with fever. Quinidine also has antipyretic properties that help cool the body down. By reducing fever and alleviating discomfort, Cinchona officinalis creams can provide relief from feverish feelings.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for feverish feeling
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Cinchona officinalis bark extract.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval.
Combine the melted oil and wax with the distilled water in a bowl, and stir until well combined.
Add the Cinchona officinalis bark extract to the mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into a glass jar.
Let the mixture cool and solidify. Store the Cinchona officinalis cream in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. Apply a small amount to the skin as needed to help reduce feverish feelings.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavoglycosides, bilobalide, and ginkgolides as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and ease discomfort. Ginkgolides also have a unique ability to inhibit platelet-activating factor (PAF), a substance that can cause inflammation and worsen feverish feelings. The antioxidants in Ginkgo biloba creams help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a sense of calm and well-being. By reducing inflammation and protecting cells, Ginkgo biloba creams can help alleviate feverish feelings and promote a more comfortable state.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for feverish feeling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Steep the dried Ginkgo biloba leaves in 2 tablespoons of hot water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid and add it to the melted mixture.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before using it as a cream to help relieve feverish feelings.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains a high concentration of biologically active constituents, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A, C, and E. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease pain. The high water content in aloe vera cream also helps to cool the skin and provide relief from heat. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of aloe-emodin and aloein can help to reduce the body's temperature, making it a helpful remedy for fever. Aloe vera's ability to soothe and calm irritated skin can also help to reduce discomfort associated with fever.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for feverish feeling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
In a separate bowl, mix the Aloe gel and shea butter until well combined.
Add the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture to the Aloe gel mixture and stir until smooth.
Add the peppermint essential oil and stir well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains curcumin, a bioactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin helps to reduce inflammation and fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Curcuma longa creams also contains other active compounds like demethoxycurcumin and bisdemethoxycurcumin, which have been shown to have similar anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The combination of these compounds in Curcuma longa creams helps to soothe and calm the body, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with fever. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Curcuma longa creams can provide relief from feverish feelings and promote a sense of well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for feverish feeling
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Add the turmeric powder and vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool and solidify. Store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains a compound called cineole, which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties. Cineole helps reduce swelling and ease congestion in the body, making it easier to breathe and relax when you have a fever. The cream also contains other compounds like terpinen-4-ol, which has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off infections that cause fever. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of Melaleuca alternifolia creams can also help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. When applied topically, the cream can help bring down a fever by reducing the body's temperature and promoting a sense of calm and comfort.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for feverish feeling
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup distilled water, 1/2 cup beeswax, 1/4 cup coconut oil, 1/4 cup olive oil, 2 tablespoons melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil.
Melt beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add olive oil and stir until well combined. Remove from heat.
Add distilled water, melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, and vitamin E oil. Stir well until the mixture cools down.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to reduce fever by fighting off the underlying infection. The flavonoids, in particular, have been shown to have antipyretic effects, meaning they directly lower body temperature. The saponins, on the other hand, have been found to have immunomodulatory properties, helping to boost the body's immune response and fight off the infection. By reducing inflammation and fighting off the infection, Sambucus nigra creams can help to alleviate fever and promote recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal creams for feverish feeling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Sambucus nigra flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat until it reaches 120°F.
Add the Sambucus nigra flowers to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers. Add 1/4 cup of beeswax and stir until melted.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool until solid. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosin, camphor, and bornyl acetate as its active constituents. These compounds have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce fever and alleviate associated discomfort. Bornyl acetate, in particular, has been shown to have a cooling effect on the body, which can help lower temperature and relieve heat-related symptoms. Camphor, on the other hand, has a numbing and analgesic effect, which can help soothe and calm the skin. By applying Rosmarinus officinalis creams, these properties can work together to provide relief from feverish feelings.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for feverish feeling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 10 drops of Rosemary essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval.
Once the mixture is melted, remove it from heat and let it cool slightly. Add the Rosemary essential oil and mix well.
Add the distilled water to the mixture and stir well. The mixture should start to thicken.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely. Once set, your Rosemary cream is ready to use. Apply it to the skin to help relieve feverish feelings.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains linalool and linalyl acetate, two active constituents with known properties. Linalool is a sedative and antispasmodic that helps reduce inflammation and relax muscles. Linalyl acetate, on the other hand, has antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections. These properties combined can help alleviate symptoms of fever, such as sweating and discomfort, by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation. The antispasmodic effects of linalool can also help soothe a sore throat and promote a restful night's sleep, further aiding in recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for feverish feeling
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat. Stir until smooth.
Add shea butter and stir until melted and smooth. Remove from heat.
Add dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and let it infuse for 2-3 hours or overnight. Strain the mixture and discard the flowers.
Add vitamin E oil and mix well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once set, your Lavandula angustifolia cream is ready to use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help ease a feverish feeling. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe a sore throat and calm the body. These teas can also help increase fluid intake, which is important when you're feeling under the weather. It's worth trying a cup when you're not feeling well.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can help with feverish feelings, drinking them every day isn't necessarily safe. Some herbs, like sage and peppermint, can be fine in moderation, but others, like echinacea and goldenseal, might cause problems if used long-term. It's best to use them sparingly and listen to your body's response.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with a feverish feeling, but it depends on the type of tea and the severity of the fever. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, can start to work within 15-30 minutes, while others, like chamomile and echinacea, might take a few hours to show results.
faq_time: If you're feeling feverish, it's best to drink herbal tea in the evening. This allows your body to relax and recover while you sleep, helping to ease your symptoms.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas can affect how your body handles fever-reducing medications. For example, if you're taking acetaminophen and drink a tea like ginseng or licorice root, it may increase the levels of the medication in your body. This can lead to potential side effects, such as liver damage or other health issues.
faq_pregnant: Herbal teas can be tempting when you're feeling feverish, but it's best to check the ingredients first. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, are generally considered safe during pregnancy. However, others, like those containing foxglove or pennyroyal, can be problematic. It's essential to choose teas that are clearly labeled as pregnancy-safe.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing relief for a feverish feeling, but it's a good idea to choose ones that are considered safe while breastfeeding. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are often used for their calming effects. However, teas with ingredients like sage and pennyroyal may not be suitable, so it's best to check the ingredients before drinking.
title_2: Beat the feverish feeling with 11 herbal creams for athletes
